title: Client portal development | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)

seo.title: Client portal development | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
seo.description: A client portal use case for companies that want one place for customer communication, customer documents, status tracking, and self-service actions.

page.title: Client portal development | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: Client portal development for communication, visibility, and self-service
page.description: A client portal use case for companies that want one place for customer communication, customer documents, status tracking, and self-service actions.

hero.title: A client portal instead of fragmented communication
hero.subtitle: Useful when end customers or clients need a clearer place for status, documents, messages, and next steps.

intro: A client portal makes sense when the same communication with customers repeats around case status, documents, service progress, or self-service actions.
Instead of email threads and manual updates, customers can use one structured interface with history, access control, and clearer next steps.

sections.title: What problem this type of app solves
body: This use case works when an important process needs to move into a clearer system with explicit roles, states, and data ownership.

sections.title: What the solution usually includes
body: The exact scope varies by company, but the recurring core is similar: roles, workflow, visibility, permissions, and integrations.
bullets: integration with CRM or internal systems

sections.title: What the system should improve
body: The goal is not to digitise old chaos. The point is to make work clearer, responsibilities sharper, and the process easier to control.
bullets: cleaner foundation for future service development

faq.question: Is a client portal relevant for a smaller company?
faq.answer: Yes, if the same customer requests and updates keep repeating and the portal can reduce support overhead.

faq.question: Does the portal need to be large from day one?
faq.answer: No. A focused first version around the most common interactions is often the best start.

faq.question: Can it connect to an existing app?
faq.answer: Yes. In most cases the portal is only valuable if it connects to existing data and workflow.

faq.question: Do we also need an internal admin side?
faq.answer: Usually yes. A useful client portal normally depends on a sensible internal backoffice layer too.

for: cleaner foundation for future service development
notFor: moving the same chaos into a new UI
